title: The AI bill that made me build TokenBar
description: Last month I opened an AI invoice and had that tiny stomach-drop moment every solo dev knows. I was using a handful of models across a bunch of tabs and tools, and the cost was not obvious until the bill showed up. Nothing was broken. It was just invisible. That bugged me enough that I started building TokenBar. TokenBar lives in the menu bar and shows token usage in real time so I can see what I am actually spending while I work. Not after the fact. Not buried in a dashboard I never check. Right there, while the context is still fresh. The lesson was not really about tokens. It was about visibility. When a cost is hidden, I ignore it. I think the same thing applies to attention, too. If my phone is buzzing, Slack is open, and a dozen browser tabs are stealing context, I do not feel distracted in some abstract way. I just feel slower. The damage shows up later in the work. That is the other reason I like building small tools. They turn vague pain into something concrete enough to fi...
